summary: Frontend-only testing of the DJ-controller MoodDeck rewrite + TypeScript DesktopShell. Executed 4 browser automation runs covering all 17 requested scenarios at viewport 1920x900. Result: 17/17 features functionally passing. No console errors. Minor design/asset concerns and one testability nit reported. Backend testing was explicitly skipped per request.

ui_bugs.issue: The image chosen for WALLPAPER_DAY (unsplash photo-1465101162946-4377e57745c3) is a milky-way / starry sky photograph, not a 'day landscape' as described in the design brief. Both day and night wallpapers therefore look nocturnal. Toggle works correctly (image src does change between day and night urls) but the daytime asset doesn't visually communicate 'day'. Consider swapping in a bright XP-Bliss-style green hillside for day.

The chibi wrap has an infinite bobAndBreathe animation that continually transforms the element. Playwright (and other automation frameworks) flag it as 'not stable', requiring force=true to click. Real users are unaffected, but this hurts testability. Consider using framer-motion transform on a nested wrapper while keeping the click target static, or apply will-change.

issues: Day wallpaper image is actually a night starfield; visually indistinguishable from 'night' theme except for hue

action_items: Replace WALLPAPER_DAY unsplash URL in /app/frontend/src/components/DesktopShell.tsx with an actual bright day-landscape image (e.g. XP Bliss / rolling green hills) so the AI Day/Night toggle is visually distinct.
(Optional) Move the bobAndBreathe animation off the click-target wrapper so automation frameworks don't flag it as unstable.
critical_code_review_comments: MoodDeck.jsx is 649 lines with Platter/Knob/Fader/main-app all in one file. Splitting Platter.jsx, Knob.jsx, Fader.jsx into the already-existing components/mooddeck/ folder would improve maintainability (Platter.jsx already exists as a separate file but MoodDeck.jsx redefines Platter inline instead of importing it — dead code / duplication).

Companion.jsx defines its own SVG-based character and its own [data-testid="companion-speech-bubble"] + companion-switch-* testids, but MoodDeck.jsx never imports it and re-implements the companion inline. Companion.jsx is now dead code and should be removed to avoid confusion for future testing agents.

onDrag handlers on the WindowFrame framer-motion component always set isDragging state on the desktop but the backdrop-blur-drop-during-drag CSS class 'is-dragging' isn't visually visible in screenshots — verify the CSS rule for .is-dragging exists and drops backdrop-blur on active windows.
The `MOODS.find(...)` calls in the recommendation card run on every render (twice). Trivial perf but consider memoizing or computing once above the JSX.

AudioRef effect uses `[track, trackIdx]` as deps but `handleNext` used inside is stale-closured on activePlaylist — since handleNext is memoized on those same deps it happens to work today, but adding `handleNext` to the effect's deps array would be cleaner.

Advanced Mixer knobs/faders update React state only — they are decorative per spec, but adding a small on-screen indicator that this is 'visual only when EQ not wired' would help users understand nothing audibly changes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 17 scenarios from iteration_2 review request pass. Verified data-testids: desktop-icon-{music-mixer,messenger}, taskbar (40px), start-button, taskbar-tab-*, window-{min,max,close,titlebar,resize}-{id}, theme-toggle, start-open-{mooddeck,retromsg}, mooddeck-app, segment-display, track-title/artist/album, audio-visualizer (has class 'w-full h-16 bg-black rounded border border-gray-800'), advanced-mixer-toggle (aria-checked toggles), mix-center (opacity-40 pointer-events-none when off), knob-{bass,mid,treble}, fader-1/2, platter-left/right (rotate via matrix transform when playing), btn-{play,prev,next,cue,sync,loop}, mood-chip-{happy,chill,study,rainy,drive,latenight,romantic,party,workout}, recommendation-card, rec-playlist-name, btn-play-playlist, mooddeck-companion, companion-character (needs force=True to click due to CSS keyframes animation), companion-speech-bubble, companion-switch-{male,female} (female applies hue-rotate(300deg) saturate(1.6) brightness(1.05) to the child img). RetroMSG e2e: input placeholder 'Type a message…', send button contains lucide 'send' icon, fake reply arrives ~2s after send. Chibi image URL loads from customer-assets.emergentagent.com. Window drag verified: dragged messenger from (180,110) to (480,250). Window maximize: 860x560→1920x860 (viewport 1920x900 with 40px taskbar).
rca of the issue: No functional issues found. Two low-priority design/testability observations: (1) The 'day' wallpaper URL is actually a night starfield — pick a truly bright landscape. (2) The chibi wrapper animation blocks automated stability checks — use force=True or refactor the animated wrapper to be a non-clickable child.
